question_en: Who is the king of the gods in Greek mythology?
context_en: Zeus is king of Olympus, god of the sky and thunder. His Roman equivalent is Jupiter.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Aphrodite?
context_en: Venus is the Roman goddess of love and beauty, equivalent to Aphrodite. The planet Venus is named after her.

question_en: Who is the god of thunder in Norse mythology?
context_en: Thor, son of Odin, is the god of thunder. His hammer Mjölnir is one of the most recognizable symbols of the Norse pantheon.

question_en: How many Olympian gods make up the main Greek pantheon?
context_en: The Twelve Olympians: Zeus, Hera, Poseidon, Demeter, Athena, Apollo, Artemis, Ares, Aphrodite, Hephaestus, Hermes and Dionysus or Hestia.

question_en: Who is the Egyptian sun god?
context_en: Ra is the sun god in ancient Egypt. He traveled the sky during the day and the underworld (Duat) at night in his barque.

question_en: Which Greek hero killed the Minotaur?
context_en: Theseus entered the Labyrinth of Crete and killed the Minotaur. He escaped guided by the thread of Ariadne, daughter of King Minos.

question_en: How many labors did Hercules perform?
context_en: Hercules (Heracles) performed 12 labors imposed by Eurystheus as penance. The first was to kill the Nemean lion.

question_en: Who is the mother of Athena?
context_en: Athena was born from Zeus's head after he devoured Metis (Wisdom), her mother, following the prophecy that she would bear powerful gods.

question_en: Which animal was Athena's favorite?
context_en: The owl symbolized Athena, goddess of wisdom. Hence the expression 'the owl of Minerva'.

question_en: Who is the god of the underworld in Greek mythology?
context_en: Hades rules the underworld. He has a three-headed dog named Cerberus that guards the entrance.

question_en: Who is the father of Zeus in Greek mythology?
context_en: Cronus (a Titan) is the father of Zeus. Cronus devoured his children for fear of being dethroned, but Rhea hid Zeus.

question_en: Which Egyptian god has a jackal head?
context_en: Anubis, god of death and embalming, has a jackal head. He guided souls to the judgment of Osiris.

question_en: Who is the trickster god in Norse mythology?
context_en: Loki is the god of deceit. Odin's adopted brother, his mischief causes Ragnarök, the end of the Norse world.

question_en: Which Titaness is the mother of Apollo and Artemis?
context_en: Leto, daughter of the Titans Coeus and Phoebe, bore Apollo and Artemis on the island of Delos, after being pursued by Hera.

question_en: Which three goddesses fought over the golden apple?
context_en: Eris threw the apple 'for the fairest'. Hera, Athena and Aphrodite fought over it, and Paris chose Aphrodite, sparking the Trojan War.

question_en: How many Muses are there in Greek mythology?
context_en: The 9 Muses are daughters of Zeus and Mnemosyne. Each protects an art: Calliope (epic), Erato (lyric), Clio (history), etc.

question_en: Which characteristic weapon does Poseidon use?
context_en: Poseidon carries a trident with which he controls the seas and causes earthquakes. His Roman equivalent is Neptune.

question_en: Who was the messenger of the Greek gods?
context_en: Hermes wears wings on his feet (sandals). He is the messenger, god of travelers, merchants and thieves. His Roman equivalent is Mercury.

question_en: Which Greek hero killed Medusa?
context_en: Perseus beheaded Medusa using a polished shield as a mirror. From her blood was born the winged horse Pegasus.

question_en: Who is the god of wine in Greek mythology?
context_en: Dionysus (Bacchus in Rome) is the god of wine, festivity and ecstasy. His followers were the maenads.

question_en: Which Roman god corresponds to Ares?
context_en: Mars is the Roman equivalent of Ares, god of war. But in Rome he had more prestige (Romulus was his son).

question_en: Which is the river of forgetfulness in the Greek underworld?
context_en: The Lethe is one of the five rivers of Hades. Drinking from it causes total forgetfulness. The others: Styx, Acheron, Cocytus and Phlegethon.

question_en: Who is the Egyptian god of the afterlife?
context_en: Osiris is lord of the afterlife. Murdered by his brother Set and resurrected by his wife Isis, he judges souls after death.

question_en: What is the name of the winged horse in Greek mythology?
context_en: Pegasus was born from the blood of Medusa when she was beheaded. Bellerophon used it to kill the Chimera.

question_en: Which Greek god is famous for his thunderbolt?
context_en: Zeus's weapon is the thunderbolt, forged by the Cyclopes. It is his symbol of power over gods and mortals.

question_en: Which animal treacherously deceived Eve in Eden?
context_en: In Genesis, the serpent tempts Eve to eat from the Tree of Knowledge. It is interpreted as Satan in later traditions.

question_en: What is the realm of the gods in Norse mythology?
context_en: Asgard is the realm of the Æsir (gods) in the Norse cosmos of the 9 worlds. It is connected to Midgard by the Bifröst bridge.

question_en: Who is the god of blacksmiths in Greek mythology?
context_en: Hephaestus, god of fire and metallurgy, forges the gods' weapons. He was lame and married to Aphrodite, who cheated on him with Ares.

question_en: What is the name of the island where Odysseus was stranded in the Odyssey?
context_en: Calypso held Odysseus on Ogygia for 7 years. After divine intervention, she let him leave for Ithaca.

question_en: Who is Achilles?
context_en: Achilles, a Greek hero, a demigod (son of Thetis). His only weak point was his heel, where his mother held him when dipping him in the Styx.

question_en: What kind of creature was the Chimera in Greek mythology?
options_en: A bull with a human body
context_en: The Chimera had the head of a lion, the body of a goat and the tail of a serpent. Bellerophon killed it with the help of Pegasus.

question_en: Which Egyptian god has a falcon head?
context_en: Horus is the sky god with a falcon head. Son of Osiris and Isis, he avenges his father's death against Set.

question_en: What is Thor's hammer?
context_en: Mjölnir is Thor's hammer, forged by the dwarves Sindri and Brokkr. It always returns to his hand and can crush mountains.

question_en: Which Greek demigod was the son of Zeus and Alcmene?
context_en: Heracles (Hercules in Rome) was the son of Zeus and the mortal Alcmene. Hera pursued him out of jealousy throughout his life.

question_en: Who created man from clay according to Greek mythology?
context_en: Prometheus molded humans from clay and gave them fire, stealing it from the gods. Zeus punished him by chaining him to the Caucasus.

question_en: What is the name of the ship Argo?
context_en: The Argo was the ship with which Jason and the Argonauts sought the Golden Fleece. It was built by Argus.

question_en: Who was the first man in Christian mythology?
context_en: According to Genesis, God created Adam from the dust of the earth. Eve was created afterward from one of his ribs.

question_en: Which Chinese god represents war?
context_en: Guan Yu was a historical general (Three Kingdoms) deified as the god of war and of business in Taoism.

question_en: Who were the Erinyes or Furies?
context_en: The Erinyes (Furies in Rome) were 3 goddesses who avenged crimes, especially crimes against the family.

question_en: Which Maya/Aztec god is the feathered serpent?
context_en: Quetzalcoatl (Aztec) or Kukulkan (Maya) is the feathered serpent, one of the most important deities of Mesoamerica.

question_en: In Hindu mythology, who is the destroyer god?
context_en: Shiva forms the Trimurti with Brahma (creator) and Vishnu (preserver). He is the god of destruction/transformation.

question_en: Which famous place did King Arthur's knights seek?
context_en: The Holy Grail (the cup of Christ) was sought by the Knights of the Round Table. Galahad or Percival find it depending on the version.

question_en: Who is the main god of the Aztecs?
context_en: Huitzilopochtli, god of war and the sun, was the patron of the Aztecs. Tenochtitlan was founded on the site he indicated.

question_en: How many heads did the Lernaean Hydra have?
options_en: Two grew for each one cut off
context_en: The Lernaean Hydra had 9 heads, and for each one cut off, 2 grew. Heracles (with Iolaus) burned them after cutting them (2nd labor).

question_en: Which Roman god corresponds to Hermes?
context_en: Mercury is the Roman equivalent of Hermes: messenger of the gods and patron of merchants and thieves.

question_en: Who is Apollo?
options_en: God of the sun, music and prophecy
context_en: Apollo is one of the twelve Olympians. God of the sun, music, poetry and oracles (especially Delphi).

question_en: What is Ragnarök in Norse mythology?
context_en: Ragnarök is the end of the Norse world: a battle in which most of the gods die (Odin, Thor, Loki, etc.).

question_en: Who was the first king of Rome according to legend?
context_en: Romulus, according to legend, founded Rome in 753 BC after killing his brother Remus. His parents were Mars and Rhea Silvia.

question_en: What did the rite of crossing the river Styx consist of?
context_en: Souls had to pay Charon, the ferryman, a coin (obol) to cross the Styx. That is why they buried the dead with coins.

question_en: What is the cosmic tree in Norse mythology?
context_en: Yggdrasil is the cosmic ash tree that connects the 9 worlds in Norse mythology.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of the hunt?
context_en: Artemis is the virgin goddess of the hunt, the moon, wild animals and young women. Twin sister of Apollo.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Artemis?
context_en: Diana is the Roman equivalent of Artemis. Goddess of the hunt, the moon and wild nature.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Athena?
context_en: Minerva is the Roman equivalent of Athena. Goddess of wisdom, the arts and strategic warfare.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Hera?
context_en: Juno is the Roman equivalent of Hera. Goddess of marriage and queen of the gods.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Zeus?
context_en: Jupiter is the Roman equivalent of Zeus. King of the gods, lord of the sky and thunder.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Poseidon?
context_en: Neptune is the Roman equivalent of Poseidon. God of the sea.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Hades?
context_en: Pluto is the Roman equivalent of Hades. God of the underworld.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Apollo?
context_en: Apollo keeps his name in Roman mythology. God of light, the arts and prophecy.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Hephaestus?
context_en: Vulcan is the Roman equivalent of Hephaestus. God of fire, volcanoes and blacksmiths.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Eros?
context_en: Cupid is the Roman equivalent of Eros. God of love, son of Venus.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Demeter?
context_en: Ceres is the Roman equivalent of Demeter. Goddess of agriculture and harvests. Hence 'cereal'.

question_en: What is the Roman equivalent of Hestia?
context_en: Vesta is the Roman equivalent of Hestia. Goddess of the hearth and the sacred fire.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of wisdom?
context_en: Athena is the Greek goddess of wisdom, the arts and strategic warfare. Daughter of Zeus.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of love?
context_en: Aphrodite is the Greek goddess of love, beauty and fertility.

question_en: What is the sacred mountain of the Greek gods?
context_en: Mount Olympus, the highest in Greece (2,917 m), was the residence of the gods according to mythology.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of the rainbow?
context_en: Iris is the messenger goddess and personification of the rainbow. She connects the sky with the earth.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of the dawn?
context_en: Eos is the Greek goddess of the dawn. In Roman mythology, her equivalent is Aurora.

question_en: Who is the Greek sun god?
options_en: All of the above in different traditions
context_en: Helios was the original sun god. Apollo later assimilated this function. Hyperion is his Titan father.

question_en: Who is the Greek goddess of the moon?
context_en: Selene is the original personification of the moon. Artemis and Hecate are also associated with it in different contexts.

question_en: Who is Hecate?
context_en: Hecate is the goddess of magic, witchcraft, ghosts and crossroads.

question_en: What was the name of the figure who opened the forbidden box in Greek mythology?
context_en: Pandora opened the 'box' (actually a jar, pithos) that contained all the evils of the world, leaving only hope inside.

question_en: Who was the first king of the gods according to the Greeks (before Zeus)?
context_en: Cronus overthrew his father Uranus. He devoured his children to avoid being dethroned. Zeus, his son, ultimately defeated him.

question_en: Which Titan holds up the sky according to mythology?
context_en: Atlas was condemned by Zeus to hold the celestial vault on his shoulders as punishment.

question_en: Who stole fire from the gods?
context_en: Prometheus stole fire from the gods to give it to humans. Zeus chained him to a rock as punishment.

question_en: What punishment did Prometheus receive?
options_en: Having an eagle devour his liver
context_en: Prometheus was chained to the Caucasus. An eagle devoured his liver every day, regenerating at night. Heracles freed him.

question_en: Which is the winged horse with a horn?
context_en: The unicorn is the horse with one horn. Pegasus is the winged horse but without a horn.

question_en: Which beings have a human torso and a horse's body?
context_en: Centaurs have a human torso and a horse's body. Chiron was the wise centaur, teacher of heroes.

question_en: Which centaur was the teacher of Achilles?
context_en: Chiron, the wisest of the centaurs, educated Achilles, Jason, Asclepius and other heroes.

question_en: Which Greek hero won the Golden Fleece?
context_en: Jason and the Argonauts traveled to Colchis in search of the Golden Fleece.

question_en: What is the name of Jason's ship?
context_en: The Argo was built by Argus with the help of Athena for Jason's voyage. The crew are the 'Argonauts'.

question_en: Who is the mother of Achilles?
context_en: Thetis, a sea nymph, was the mother of Achilles. She dipped him in the Styx to make him invulnerable, except for the heel.

question_en: What is the 'Achilles' heel'?
context_en: The Achilles heel, where Thetis held him, was his only vulnerable point. An arrow from Paris there killed him.

question_en: Who killed Achilles in the Trojan War?
context_en: Paris (a Trojan prince), guided by Apollo, shot the arrow that killed Achilles by striking his heel.

question_en: Who is the father of Hector and Paris?
context_en: Priam, king of Troy, was the father of Hector, Paris, Cassandra and 50 other children.

question_en: Who is the prophetess of Troy whose prophecies no one believed?
context_en: Cassandra, daughter of Priam, received from Apollo the gift of prophecy but also the curse of not being believed.

question_en: Who abducted Helen from Sparta?
context_en: Paris abducted Helen, wife of King Menelaus, provoking the Trojan War.

question_en: Who is the author of 'The Iliad' and 'The Odyssey'?
context_en: Homer (8th century BC) is the traditional author of 'The Iliad' and 'The Odyssey'.

question_en: How many years did Odysseus wander after the Trojan War?
context_en: According to the Odyssey, Odysseus (Ulysses) took 10 years to return to Ithaca after the Trojan War.

question_en: Who is the wife of Odysseus?
context_en: Penelope, wife of Odysseus, patiently waited for her husband for 20 years, weaving and unweaving a shroud.

question_en: Who is the son of Odysseus and Penelope?
context_en: Telemachus, son of Odysseus, was the protagonist of the first four books of the Odyssey (the 'Telemachy').

question_en: Which Cyclops did Odysseus encounter?
context_en: Odysseus blinded the Cyclops Polyphemus, son of Poseidon, which provoked the enmity of the sea god.

question_en: Who is the sorceress who turned Odysseus's men into pigs?
context_en: Circe, a sorceress of the island of Aeaea, transformed Odysseus's crew into pigs.

question_en: Who held Odysseus for 7 years on an island?
context_en: The nymph Calypso held Odysseus on the island of Ogygia for 7 years, offering him immortality.

question_en: Which dangerous creatures lured sailors with their song?
context_en: The Sirens lured sailors with their irresistible song, driving them to shipwreck.

question_en: How did Odysseus escape the Sirens' song?
options_en: He tied himself to the mast
context_en: Odysseus plugged his sailors' ears with wax and had himself tied to the mast so he could hear the song without succumbing.

question_en: Who are Scylla and Charybdis?
context_en: Scylla was a multi-headed monster and Charybdis a whirlpool. They were in a strait (Messina?) through which Odysseus had to pass.

question_en: Who is Oedipus?
options_en: King of Thebes who killed his father and married his mother
context_en: Oedipus, king of Thebes, unknowingly killed his father Laius and married his mother Jocasta, fulfilling a prophecy.

question_en: Which famous riddle did Oedipus solve?
context_en: Oedipus solved the riddle of the Sphinx of Thebes: 'What animal walks on 4 legs in the morning, 2 at noon and 3 at night?'. Answer: the human being.

question_en: Which god condemned Sisyphus to push a rock eternally?
context_en: Sisyphus was condemned by Zeus to push a rock to the top of a mountain, where it always rolled back down.

question_en: Who is Tantalus?
options_en: Condemned to never drink or eat
context_en: Tantalus, condemned by the gods, was submerged in water up to his neck but could not drink, with fruit above his head but could not eat it.

question_en: Which Greek hero defeated the Amazons?
context_en: Heracles (Hercules), in his 9th labor, obtained the belt of the Amazon queen Hippolyta.

question_en: Who is the queen of the Amazons in mythology?
context_en: Hippolyta and Penthesilea are two famous Amazon queens, in different episodes of the myth.

question_en: Which Egyptian god is the husband of Isis?
context_en: Osiris is the husband and brother of Isis. He was murdered by his brother Set and resurrected by Isis.

question_en: Who is the Egyptian sky god, son of Isis and Osiris?
context_en: Horus, with a falcon head, is the son of Isis and Osiris. Avenger of his father, associated with royalty.

question_en: Who is Anubis?
context_en: Anubis, with a jackal head, is the god of mummification and guardian of the dead in the Egyptian pantheon.

question_en: Who is Thoth?
options_en: Egyptian god of writing and wisdom
context_en: Thoth, with an ibis head, is the Egyptian god of writing, wisdom and the moon.

question_en: Which Egyptian god murdered his brother Osiris?
context_en: Set, god of chaos and the deserts, murdered his brother Osiris out of jealousy for the throne of Egypt.

question_en: Who is Bastet?
options_en: Egyptian goddess with a cat's head
context_en: Bastet, with a cat's head, was the Egyptian goddess of the home, fertility and cats.

question_en: Who is Sekhmet?
context_en: Sekhmet, with a lioness head, was the Egyptian goddess of war, disease and healing.

question_en: Who is the king of the Norse gods?
context_en: Odin, father of Thor and Loki's adoptive brother, is the king of the Norse gods. He resides in Asgard.

question_en: How many eyes does Odin have?
context_en: Odin sacrificed one of his eyes at Mimir's well in exchange for wisdom.

question_en: What are the names of Odin's ravens?
context_en: Hugin ('thought') and Munin ('memory') are Odin's ravens. They fly around the world and bring him news.

question_en: How many legs does Sleipnir, Odin's horse, have?
context_en: Sleipnir, son of Loki, has 8 legs. He is the fastest horse and travels through all the worlds.

question_en: Who is Frigg?
context_en: Frigg, wife of Odin, is the Norse goddess of marriage, motherhood and domestic wisdom.

question_en: Who is Loki's daughter who rules the realm of the Norse dead?
context_en: Hel is Loki's daughter. She rules the realm of the same name, where the dead who did not fall in battle go.

question_en: Who is Freyja in Norse mythology?
context_en: Freyja is the Norse goddess of love, beauty, fertility and magic (seidr). She also receives half of those who die in battle.

question_en: What is the realm of the giants in Norse mythology?
context_en: Jotunheim is the land of the jotuns (giants). Asgard is that of the Æsir gods. Midgard is that of humans.

question_en: What is the name of the bridge that connects Asgard with Midgard?
context_en: Bifröst is the rainbow bridge that connects Asgard (realm of the gods) with Midgard (world of humans).

question_en: Who is Heimdall?
context_en: Heimdall, son of nine mothers, guards the Bifröst against the giants. He will blow the horn Gjallarhorn at the start of Ragnarök.

question_en: Who is the god of fertility and crops in Norse mythology?
context_en: Freyr, brother of Freyja, is the god of fertility, prosperity and the sun.

question_en: Which wolf will kill Odin at Ragnarök?
context_en: Fenrir, a giant wolf and son of Loki, will devour Odin during Ragnarök.

question_en: Which serpent surrounds the world in Norse mythology?
context_en: Jörmungandr, the 'World Serpent' (Midgardsormr), is a child of Loki. It surrounds Midgard biting its own tail.

question_en: Who will kill Jörmungandr at Ragnarök?
context_en: Thor will kill Jörmungandr at Ragnarök, but will die poisoned after 9 steps.

question_en: Which Hindu god is known as 'the destroyer'?
context_en: Shiva is one of the three main gods of Hinduism, known as 'the destroyer' or 'the transformer'.

question_en: Which Hindu god is the 'preserver'?
context_en: Vishnu, the preserver, incarnates in various avatars (like Rama, Krishna and Buddha) to restore dharma.

question_en: Which Hindu god is the 'creator'?
context_en: Brahma is the creator god in the Hindu Trimurti (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva).

question_en: Who is Ganesha?
options_en: Hindu god with an elephant head
context_en: Ganesha, son of Shiva and Parvati, has an elephant head. He is the god of beginnings, remover of obstacles.

question_en: Which Hindu goddess is the wife of Shiva?
context_en: Parvati is the wife of Shiva. Mother of Ganesha and Kartikeya. She manifests as Durga or Kali in her warrior aspects.

question_en: Which Hindu goddess is the wife of Vishnu?
context_en: Lakshmi is the wife of Vishnu and the goddess of prosperity, wealth, fortune and beauty.

question_en: Which Maya/Aztec god represents the sun and war?
context_en: Huitzilopochtli was the main solar and war god of the Aztecs. Patron of the city of Tenochtitlan.

question_en: Which Maya/Aztec god is the god of rain?
context_en: Tlaloc was the Mexica god of rain and fertility. The equivalent of the Maya Chaac.

question_en: Who is the Maya/Aztec god of the underworld?
context_en: Mictlantecuhtli was the god of the underworld (Mictlan) in Mexica/Aztec mythology.

question_en: Which Aztec goddess was the mother of Huitzilopochtli?
context_en: Coatlicue ('the one with the serpent skirt') was the mother of Huitzilopochtli and goddess of fertility and the earth.

question_en: What is the Popol Vuh?
context_en: The Popol Vuh is the sacred book of the K'iche' Maya people. It contains the creation of the world and the exploits of the divine twins.

question_en: Which is the main god of the Incas?
context_en: Inti, the sun, was the main god of the Incas. Viracocha was the creator god. Pachamama, the mother earth.

question_en: What does Pachamama represent?
context_en: Pachamama is the mother earth in Andean religions. Goddess of fertility, the harvest and nature.

question_en: Who was Brahma in Hindu mythology?
context_en: Brahma is the creator god in the Hindu trinity (Trimurti).

question_en: What is the Mahabharata?
context_en: The Mahabharata is one of the great Hindu epic texts (~100,000 verses). It contains the Bhagavad Gita.

question_en: Which Chinese god is the 'Jade Emperor'?
context_en: The Jade Emperor (Yu Huang) is the supreme ruler of heaven in traditional Chinese mythology.

question_en: Which divine animal is the guardian of the east in China?
context_en: The Azure Dragon guards the east in Chinese cosmology. The others: White Tiger (west), Vermilion Bird (south), Black Tortoise (north).

question_en: Which Japanese dragon is famous for being killed by Susanoo?
context_en: Yamata no Orochi was an 8-headed dragon. Susanoo got it drunk on sake before killing it.

question_en: Who is Amaterasu?
context_en: Amaterasu Ōmikami is the sun goddess in Shinto. The Japanese imperial family claims to descend from her.

question_en: What is a kitsune?
options_en: A fox spirit in Japanese folklore
context_en: Kitsune are fox spirits from Japanese folklore. They can have up to 9 tails and possess magical powers.

question_en: What is the cosmic tree of the Celts?
context_en: The oak was the sacred tree par excellence for the Celts. The word 'druid' derives from 'dru' (oak).

question_en: Who is King Arthur?
context_en: Arthur is a legendary British king who defends his land from the Saxons. A symbol of the chivalric ideal.

question_en: What is the name of King Arthur's sword?
context_en: Excalibur is the legendary sword of King Arthur, given by the Lady of the Lake.

question_en: Who is Merlin?
context_en: Merlin is the wizard, prophet and advisor of King Arthur in the Arthurian legends.

question_en: Which legendary island is a paradise in Celtic mythology?
context_en: Tír na nÓg, Avalon and Annwn are different names for paradisiacal 'otherworlds' in Celtic mythologies.

question_en: Which Egyptian god had a crocodile head?
context_en: Sobek, god of the Nile and of crocodiles, was worshipped in places like Kom Ombo.

question_en: Which is the sacred river of the Hindus?
context_en: The Ganges (Ganga) is the sacred river of Hinduism. Bathing in it purifies sins according to tradition.

question_en: Who is Hanuman?
options_en: A Hindu god in the shape of a monkey
context_en: Hanuman is the Hindu monkey god, a symbol of strength, devotion and courage. He accompanied Rama in the Ramayana.

question_en: Which Celtic god was the 'Good God' according to the Irish?
context_en: The Dagda was the 'Good God' in Celtic-Irish mythology, leader of the Tuatha Dé Danann.

question_en: Who is Cú Chulainn?
context_en: Cú Chulainn is the legendary hero of the Ulster Cycle in Irish mythology.
